description: Temporal hold-out following the TESSERACT temporally consistent protocol: train on 2012-2018 records (~53,000), test on 2019-2021 records (~23,350), no post-2018 fine-tuning (paper tab:temporal). mean +/- std over seeds {7, 42, 123, 777, 2024}. AIPR/FRR only defined for runtime-capable methods.

protocol.notes: One split ending 2021; Android 12-14 changes and post-2021 malware outside its reach. Drift-aware detectors not run in adaptive mode. MaskDroid temporal behavior not evaluated.

key_findings: TrustGuard's AUROC falls 2.2 points across the gap (0.963 -> 0.941), the smallest degradation among evaluated methods.
DexRay, statistically tied with TrustGuard on the full set, degrades 5.6 points (0.961 -> 0.905).
TrustGuard retains the highest enforcement coverage under the shift (55.2% AIPR at 2.6% FRR).
